reasoning: The recent stock action shows clear downward short-term momentum, falling from $48.88 on March 10 to $45.57 on March 14. This is supported by negative fundamental news, primarily weak 2025 guidance ($4-$4.75 adjusted EPS) due to soft agricultural market margins and biofuel policy uncertainty detailed in their February earnings report. Combining the short-term negative trend with the statistical linear model (P = 0.48 for > 45.57) gives a slightly bearish short-term outlook. However, as the forecast horizon expands, high uncertainty, typical long-term positive equity drift, ongoing stock buybacks (100M extended program), and relative undervaluation (P/E ~10x) pull the probabilities back towards and eventually slightly above the 0.5 baseline, reflecting mean reversion from current multi-year lows.
